text: Common refactorings: Extract Method , Rename Variable, Move Method, Replace Conditional with Polymorphism, Introduce Parameter Object

Refactoring (Martin Fowler, 1999) improves code structure without changing external behavior. It's a disciplined technique for cleaning up code, reducing technical debt, and making future changes easier. Refactoring is NOT rewriting — it's a series of small, safe transformations with tests as safety net.
Common refactorings: Extract Method (break large function), Rename Variable, Move Method, Replace Conditional with Polymorphism, Introduce Parameter Object.
Code smells (indicators): Long Method, Large Class, Duplicate Code, Feature Envy, Primitive Obsession.
